paragraphs: After experiencing the full spectrum of development—from hands-on engineering to management—as a core team member, I took the leap and started my freelance journey in 2026. While I used to be based in Tokyo, the freedom from location constraints has allowed me to relocate to the beautiful city of Matsumoto in Nagano Prefecture, where I now operate.
My core philosophy is to 'work with joy.' I truly believe that for me to enjoy what I do, the team must be having fun, and our users must enjoy the journey just as much.

This site feels more like a space for self-expression than a traditional portfolio. If anything here resonates with you, please feel free to reach out—whether it's for a casual chat, shared interests, or a project. (Though, I certainly won't complain if it's about a work opportunity!)

berserk.label: Beherit will cry soon - Berserk Style

hokuto.alt: Fist of the North Star-style profile image
hokuto.label: No sleeves needed - Fist of the North Star Style

launch-website.description: It’s been a while since I built something purely for the fun of it. Reminds me of why developing is so much fun.

vue-spring-boot-project.description: Grateful to be getting hands-on work purely as a coder right now. Having a blast with it.

start-freelancing.description: Starting something new is just plain fun. Finding that perfect sweet spot for tax optimization without overdoing it felt incredibly satisfying.

moved-to-matsumoto.description: Since location no longer tied me down, I thought, 'Why not live near the ski resorts?' and made the move. Being a city boy at heart, I looked for an urban area close to the mountains, though it turns out it’s still a bit of a trek to go every single weekend. That said, living in a beautiful city surrounded by mountains is truly amazing.

start-skiing.description: A friend dragged me into it, and I instantly unlocked it as a lifelong obsession. Currently aiming for the Olympics.

join-consulting-firm.description: I never thought I’d end up working in one of those shiny, glamorous corporate offices. But in reality, it was a world where 'might makes right.' Honestly, it was fun in its own way.

join-salesforce-partner-firm.description: The company was packed with brilliant engineers. Looking back after finishing a massive, long-term system development, I sometimes think, 'Wait, couldn't I have just built this alone?'—and this company was actually capable of doing exactly that. Well, until we got acquired by a mega-corporation. Also, I didn't really like Salesforce.

programming-instructor.description: While staying at the same company, I somehow ended up doing rookie training for system development, teaching at a kids' coding school, and handling marketing.

long-term-on-site-in-ishigaki-island.description: I thought I joined an IT company, but somehow we ended up building a hotel on Ishigaki Island. I lived there on and off for about a year and a half. It was truly, deeply an incredible place.

start-golfing.description: Apparently, if you break into the world's top 50, you get into the Masters. So that's the goal. Right now, I'm probably ranked around 43.2 millionth.

start-career.description: I was just living a carefree life as a freelancer/part-timer, but then I realized I’d never done an office job. I decided to look for work using my hobby of programming, and it turned out I could actually pull it off.

contact.intro: Thank you for visiting. For project inquiries, consultations, or any comments, please feel free to reach out using the form below.
